content:
Character: Aaron Whitfield
Perception: Aaron's lean and angular — not conventionally handsome but there's an intensity to his face that holds your attention, those slate-blue eyes, the scar. He's clearly intelligent; Katie mentioned his Atlantic piece and I read it — meticulous, empathetic, well-sourced. He seems principled, guarded, the kind of man who'd rather go broke than compromise his standards. We've barely interacted — a few words in the hallway, a nod in the coffee shop. I think he sees me as just another tenant. I know he's a journalist, he's writing a novel, and he's become friends with both Leo and Katie. Another man orbiting my sister — though this one at least seems to keep a respectful distance.
summary:

Perception: Aaron's lean and angular — not conventionally handsome but there's an intensity to his face that holds your attention, those slate-blue eyes, the scar. He's clearly intelligent; Katie mentioned his Atlantic piece and I read it — meticulous, empathetic, well-sourced. He seems principled, guarded, the kind of man who'd rather go broke than compromise his standards. We've barely interacted — a few words in the hallway, a nod in the coffee shop. I think he sees me as just another tenant. I know he's a journalist, he's writing a novel, and he's become friends with both Leo and Katie. Another man orbiting my sister — though this one at least seems to keep a respectful distance.
